purpose: Canonical taxonomy for judging source quality, claim quality, and public-facing site text across six policy perspectives.

classifications.label: Source fact
classifications.description: Use when the statement is presented as grounded in a source document, source quote, explicit table item, or directly verified timeline moment.
classifications.editorial_rule: Keep the wording close to what the source establishes. Attribute lower-authority sources explicitly.

classifications.label: Interpretation
classifications.description: Use when the content synthesizes multiple sources, current interpretation outputs, or review logic into a best-current reading.
classifications.editorial_rule: Present as current interpretation, synthesis, or reading of the source base. Do not overstate certainty.

classifications.label: Local gap
classifications.description: Use when a national or regional requirement, expectation, or direction is visible, but explicit local adoption or local documentation is not yet visible in Almere or Flevoland sources.
classifications.editorial_rule: Frame as an adoption, documentation, or explicitation gap, not as a database error.

classifications.label: Human choice question
classifications.description: Use when the source base raises a real decision, sequencing choice, interpretation need, or local design choice that the system should not decide automatically.
classifications.editorial_rule: Phrase as a possible decision, possible follow-up action, or point for human review. Do not present as already decided policy.

perspectives.title: Norm
perspectives.question: What is required, allowed, expected, or binding?
perspectives.description: Used for statements about obligations, agreed arrangements, allowed room, or norm-setting interpretations.

normative_status_values.meaning: Formeel verplichtend of bindend volgens broncategorie en normatieve bronpassage.
Afspraak of akkoordtekst; bestuurlijk relevant maar niet automatisch een wettelijke plicht voor Almere.
Toelichting, format, handreiking, advies of praktische uitleg.
Lagere-autoriteitsbron met normachtige taal; altijd expliciet toeschrijven.

perspectives.editorial_rule: Differentiate clearly between binding text, agreed text, lower-authority interpretation, and practical guidance.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not let commentary, FAQ text, sector pages, or summaries sound more binding than the stronger source base allows.

perspectives.title: Time
perspectives.question: Which dates, deadlines, phasing, review moments, and budget-calendar alignments matter?
perspectives.description: Used for dated policy events, milestones, expected publication moments, review cycles, and planning dependencies.
perspectives.editorial_rule: Keep dates chronological, distinguish expected moments from formal deadlines, and surface source-backed timing before local interpretation.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not invent internal Almere timing if the source base only gives external milestones.

perspectives.title: Money
perspectives.question: Which funding flows, budget windows, allocation conditions, and co-financing logic apply?
perspectives.description: Used for budget windows, SPUK logic, funding channels, allocation conditions, and financial dependencies.
perspectives.editorial_rule: State what the source says about funding routes, conditions, and windows; keep unverified financial assumptions out of the public layer.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not present rumored carry-over rules, local budgets, or scenarios as facts unless a source or verified calculation supports them.

perspectives.title: Governance
perspectives.question: Who decides, who coordinates, who owns, and who must approve?
perspectives.description: Used for ownership, approval, coordination, regional roles, and decision routes.
perspectives.editorial_rule: Separate decision-making, coordination, execution, and accountability roles wherever the source base allows.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not collapse actors into vague labels like 'the region' if the source is more specific.

perspectives.title: Locality
perspectives.question: What matters specifically for Almere or Flevoland?
perspectives.description: Used for explicitly local or regional relevance, adoption gaps, and public local translation.
perspectives.editorial_rule: Show clearly whether something is explicitly local, regionally translated, or only nationally visible so far.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not imply that Almere has formally adopted a line if the local public source base does not yet show that.

perspectives.title: Execution
perspectives.question: What must policymakers actually organize, prepare, or sequence?
perspectives.description: Used for operational follow-up, sequencing questions, workagenda preparation, and implementation dependencies.
perspectives.editorial_rule: Phrase execution as what must be organized, clarified, prepared, or sequenced, while keeping ownership and decision status explicit.
perspectives.exclusion_rule: Do not turn the generator into a policymaker by inventing staffing, internal milestones, or implementation advice.
